Name: Hyprstream
Author: danmaxis
Description: Hyprland control surface for Stream Deck-class hardware: live workspaces, window state with on/off feedback, audio/mic with PipeWire state polling, capture (screenshot + screen recording), MPRIS media keys with album art, DnD toggle, monitor swap, and six system-info displays (clock, CPU, RAM, battery, temperature, uptime).

Actions.Name: Workspace Focus
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to focus a Hyprland workspace. Pick the destination type in the Property Inspector: a numeric workspace (1..10), a named one, a special workspace (toggles the overlay on/off — perfect for scratchpads), the default scratchpad, or a relative selector (e+1, r-1, previous, etc.). The icon reflects live state for numeric and special destinations: filled with the accent color when active/visible, dim with a count indicator when busy, muted when empty.

Actions.Name: Move Window to Workspace
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to send the focused window to the configured destination. Pick the destination type in the Property Inspector: numeric (1..10), named, special workspace (overlay), the default scratchpad, or a relative selector (e+1, r-1, previous, etc.). The move is silent by default — the window goes to the destination but you stay on your current workspace; enable 'follow focus' to switch along with the window.

Actions.Name: Mic Mute
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to toggle the default microphone mute via PipeWire. Icon shows live state: green with an open mic glyph when live, red with a slash when muted. Mirrors mute changes from your keyboard or other apps within ~500 ms. Targets @DEFAULT_AUDIO_SOURCE@. Requires: wpctl (pacman -S wireplumber).

Actions.Name: Speaker Mute
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to toggle the default audio sink mute. Icon shows the current volume as a percentage and turns red when muted. Mirrors mute changes from XF86 keys or any tool that changes the sink — the deck stays in sync. Pair with two Volume Step keys (+5/-5) for a complete deck-side audio control. Requires: wpctl (pacman -S wireplumber).

Actions.Name: Volume Step
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to step the default audio sink volume up or down by the configured percentage. Pair two keys (one with delta +5 and another with -5) for a deck-side volume rocker. Icon shows the step delta on top and the current volume below; the icon turns red while the sink is muted. Requires: wpctl (pacman -S wireplumber).

Actions.Name: Focus Direction
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to move keyboard focus to the next window in the configured direction (left, right, up, or down). Calls hyprctl dispatch movefocus — same behaviour as your Super+Arrow keybinds, but on the deck. Configure four separate keys for full directional focus from the deck.

Actions.Name: Close Window
Actions.Tooltip: Close window(s). 'Active' mode closes just the focused window via hyprctl killactive — same as Alt+F4, the app gets to prompt about unsaved changes. 'Workspace' mode enumerates every client on the current workspace and sends each a graceful close. By default Workspace mode requires tap-twice-to-confirm (3s window): first tap arms the key and draws a draining red ring around the X; second tap inside the window fires the close, otherwise it disarms silently. Confirmation is configurable in the property inspector. A red ALL badge in workspace mode makes the two modes visually distinct.

Actions.Name: Window Toggle
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to toggle a window state (float / maximize / fullscreen / fake-fullscreen / pin) on the focused window. The icon's ON/OFF label reflects the current state — change focus to a different window and the icon updates within one event-socket round-trip. Note: fake-fullscreen state isn't reflected (Hyprland doesn't expose it cleanly), so that mode always shows OFF until pressed.

Actions.Name: Swap Monitor Workspaces
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to swap the workspace on the active monitor with the one on a neighbouring monitor (left, right, up, or down). Useful for moving the entire current workspace's contents to another screen wholesale, without dragging windows one by one. Wraps hyprctl dispatch swapactiveworkspaces current <direction>.

Actions.Name: Resize Active Window
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to grow or shrink the focused window in the configured direction by a fixed pixel amount. Left/Right resize the horizontal dimension (left = shrink, right = grow); Up/Down resize the vertical dimension (up = shrink, down = grow). Set the per-key pixel step in the property inspector (default 80). Wraps hyprctl dispatch resizeactive — works in both tiled and floating modes.

Actions.Name: Swap Window with Neighbor
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to swap the focused window with its neighbour in the configured direction (left, right, up, or down) within the current workspace. Configure four keys for full directional swap from the deck. Wraps hyprctl dispatch swapwindow.

Actions.Name: Diagnostics
Actions.Tooltip: Live health indicator for the plugin's three connections to Hyprland: env (HYPRLAND_INSTANCE_SIGNATURE / XDG_RUNTIME_DIR), socket (.socket2.sock), and hyprctl. Green/yellow/red dots reflect status; the bottom label says how the instance was discovered (env vs. discovery). Tap the key to dump a verbose snapshot of resolved paths, last hyprctl stderr, last refresh error, and Hyprland version to the plugin logs — useful for debugging a freshly-broken 0.55 install.

Actions.Name: Live Config Tweak
Actions.Tooltip: Toggle a Hyprland config keyword live, no reload. Pick a preset (gaps, border, rounding, blur, glow, animations, cursor size, cursor zoom, dim inactive) or enter a custom keyword + value list to cycle through. The icon reads the current value from Hyprland on every press, so it always reflects reality. Requires Hyprland 0.55+ (uses the Lua /eval and /keyword socket commands).

Actions.Name: Presentation Mode
Actions.Tooltip: One-tap presentation/demo mode. Boosts cursor size + zoom, dims inactive windows, optionally disables animations — everything an audience needs to follow your screen. A second tap restores the exact original values. State is held in plugin memory, so the restore is precise. Requires Hyprland 0.55+.

Actions.Name: Recording Toggle
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to start or stop a screen recording via wf-recorder. Region/full/full-audio modes available. Icon shows a pulsing red dot while recording — both as a visual indicator and so you don't forget you're recording. Recording state survives plugin restarts (PID is tracked in $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/hyprstream-deck/record.pid). Output saved to ~/Videos/screencast-<timestamp>.mp4. Requires: wf-recorder, slurp (pacman -S wf-recorder slurp).

Actions.Name: Screenshot
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to capture a screenshot via grim/slurp/wl-copy. Region mode prompts you to drag a selection and copies to the clipboard; full mode captures the focused output to clipboard; full-file mode also saves to ~/Pictures/Screenshots/screenshot-<timestamp>.png. Fire-and-forget — the icon stays static. Requires: grim, slurp, wl-copy (pacman -S grim slurp wl-clipboard).

Actions.Name: Do Not Disturb
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to toggle Do-Not-Disturb on your notification daemon (mako or dunst — auto-detected). When DnD is on, notifications are silenced. Icon mirrors state (red bell + slash when DnD is on; green bell when alerts are flowing) and updates within one second of any external change. Handy before going live, in meetings, or during deep-focus sessions. Requires: makoctl (pacman -S mako) OR dunstctl (pacman -S dunst).

Actions.Name: Media Control
Actions.Tooltip: Tap to control the active MPRIS media player (Spotify, browsers, mpv, VLC, etc.) via playerctl. Modes: play-pause / next / previous. The play-pause icon reflects whether something is currently playing (▶ when paused, ❚❚ when playing) and updates within ~one second of state changes from anywhere. With Show Album Art enabled, local file:// art is composited under the glyph; HTTPS art (Spotify/Tidal) loads when 'Allow remote fetch' is also on. Requires: playerctl (pacman -S playerctl).

Actions.Name: Clock
Actions.Tooltip: Live time display. 24h or 12h format with optional seconds and date. Refreshes every second when seconds are shown, otherwise every 30 seconds. Display-only — pressing the key just acknowledges the press, no system action. Works on any Linux setup (no Hyprland required).

Actions.Name: CPU Usage
Actions.Tooltip: System-wide CPU utilization, read from /proc/stat at ~1.5s intervals. Color goes orange past the warn threshold (default 70%) and red past the crit threshold (default 90%). A progress bar at the bottom mirrors the percentage. Display-only. Works on any Linux setup (no Hyprland required).

Actions.Name: RAM Usage
Actions.Tooltip: Memory pressure, read from /proc/meminfo (MemTotal/MemAvailable). Refreshes every 2s. Label shows total RAM in GB when detected. Color thresholds at warn (default 75%) and crit (default 90%). Display-only. Works on any Linux setup.

Actions.Name: Battery
Actions.Tooltip: Laptop battery charge, read from /sys/class/power_supply/BAT*. Auto-detects the first BAT entry; override the name in the property inspector if needed. Shows a charging-bolt glyph when on AC, turns red at/under the warn threshold (default 20%). Refreshes every 30s. Display-only.

Actions.Name: Temperature
Actions.Tooltip: CPU/SoC temperature in °C, read from /sys/class/thermal/<zone>/temp. Default zone is thermal_zone0; switch zones in the PI if your laptop reports the right sensor elsewhere. Color thresholds at warn (75°) and crit (90°). Refreshes every 2s. Display-only.

Actions.Name: Uptime
Actions.Tooltip: System uptime, read from /proc/uptime. Two formats: 'short' (3d4h, 12h34m, 7m) and 'human' (3d 4h). Refreshes every minute. Display-only. Works on any Linux setup.
